concept: Endure / Last Through Time / Persist Despite Difficulty
description: To suffer through something painful or difficult; to remain in existence over time. 'Pheru' names the quality of lasting—persistence through challenge, continuation despite pressure.
sound_symbolism: The word holds through. 'Phe' /ɸe/ begins with steady breath, presence maintained. Then 'ru' rolls onward, endurance continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb or noun: 'mi pheru' (I endure), 'lo pheru' (endurance). Related to 'thuroi' (resilient) but emphasizes duration rather than recovery.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Endurance is not mere suffering—it is conscious persistence with awareness, neither denying difficulty nor being overwhelmed.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Endurance determined survival—the capacity to persist through winter, drought, hardship until conditions changed.
